krama: नमो॑ वास्त॒व्या॑य । वास्त॒व्या॑य च । च वास्तु॒पाय॑ । वास्तु॒पाय॑ च । च इति॑ च ॥

sayana.english:
'Vastu' is wealth in the form of cattle, horses, and other objects; he who exists there as those objects is 'vastavyah'. 'Vastu' is land for building a house; its protector is 'vastupah'. (16)

|| Thus in the Vedartha-prakasha, the commentary on the Yajur-samhita composed by Sayanacharya-Madhava, in the fourth Kanda, fifth Prapathaka, the commentary on the Sri Rudram, ends the Seventh Anuvaka. ||

bhatta_bhaskara.english:
'Namah vastavyaya cha'. 'Vastu' is wealth like cattle, horses, etc.; to him existing as those effects. Or: to him existing in the building-site (vastu). 'Vastupaya cha'—one who protects (pa-ti) the site. [Grammatical note]. Or to the Master of the dwelling. As it is said: "Salutation to Rudra, the Lord of the Dwelling (vastoshpati)." (16)

Now for the Purashcharana of this anuvaka: having fasted for one day and chanting eight hundred times, one becomes a perfected practitioner. By this, one desiring the control of the four castes should offer ten thousand sticks of Ashvattha, Udumbara, Vata, and Plaksha wood, soaked in curd, honey, and ghee; they will then be in his control. One desiring intelligence (medha) should offer five thousand oblations of Kapila-cow ghee; he becomes intelligent. One desiring nourishment (pushti) should offer nine thousand cakes (apupa). One desiring health...
...

abhinava_shankara.sanskrit:
सप्तमोऽनुवाकः ॥

                         ———

      नमो दुन्दुभ्याय चाहनन्याय च नमो
      घृष्णवे च प्रमृशाय च नमो दूताय च
      प्रहिताय च नमो निषङ्गिणे चेषुधिमते च
      नमस्तीक्ष्णेषवे चायुधिने च नमः स्वायु-
      धाय च सुधन्वने च नमः स्रुत्याय च
      पथ्याय च नमः काश्याय च नीप्याय च
      नमः सूद्याय च सरस्याय च नमो ना-
      याय च वैशन्ताय च नमः कूप्याय चा-
      वट्याय च नमो वर्ष्याय चावर्ष्याय च
      नमो मेघ्याय च विद्युत्याय च नमः ई-
      ध्रियायें चातप्याय च नमो बाल्याय च
      रेघिमयाय च नमो वास्तव्याय च वा-
      स्तुपाय च ॥
abhinava_shankara.english:
Seventh Anuvāka (saptamo'nuvākaḥ)

                         ———

      Homage to Dundubhya and to Āhananya; homage
      to Ghṛṣṇave and to Pramṛśa; homage to Dūta and to
      Prahita; homage to Niṣaṅgiṇe and to Iṣudhimate;
      homage to Tīkṣṇeṣave and to Āyudhine; homage to Svāyu-
      dha and to Sudhanvane; homage to Srutyā and to
      Pathyā; homage to Kāśya and to Nīpya;
      homage to Sūdya and to Sarasya; homage to Nā-
      dya and to Vaiśanta; homage to Kūpya and to
      Avaṭya; homage to Varṣya and to Avarṣya;
      homage to Meghya and to Vidyutya; homage to
      Īdhriya and to Ātapya; homage to Vātya and to
      Rebhya; homage to Vāstavya and to
      Vāstupa.
